title: New Solar Tariff Policy Sparks Industry Debate
text: The federal government announced a revised solar tariff policy that reduces import duties on photovoltaic panels by 15 percent. Industry groups say the policy will accelerate rooftop solar adoption in residential markets. Critics argue the policy undercuts domestic solar panel manufacturers who cannot compete on price.

title: Solar Energy Grid Integration Reaches New Milestone
text: Grid operators reported that solar energy now accounts for 12 percent of peak electricity generation nationwide. The milestone was reached thanks to improved battery storage policy incentives and streamlined permitting for utility-scale solar farms. Analysts predict solar will surpass wind energy capacity by 2028.

title: Climate Policy Framework Updated for 2026
text: Legislators passed a comprehensive climate policy framework that sets binding emission reduction targets for the energy sector. The framework includes subsidies for solar and wind projects alongside carbon pricing mechanisms. Environmental groups praised the policy but called for faster implementation timelines.

title: Electric Vehicle Battery Costs Drop Below $100 per kWh
text: Battery manufacturers achieved the long-anticipated milestone of producing lithium-ion cells below 100 dollars per kilowatt hour. The cost reduction is driven by new cathode chemistry and manufacturing scale. Automakers expect the lower costs to bring electric vehicle prices to parity with combustion engines within two years.

title: AI Models Optimize Solar Farm Output by 20 Percent
text: A machine learning system deployed across 50 solar farms increased energy output by an average of 20 percent through real-time panel angle optimization. The policy implications are significant as grid operators consider mandating smart optimization for new solar installations. Researchers note the approach works equally well for wind turbine positioning.
